step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the assessment provided by @Agent1 regarding the selection of option B - ACE inhibitors/enalapril being unsafe during pregnancy. One additional point supports this view; several large-scale epidemiological studies consistently show an increased incidence of specific malformations associated with fetal exposure to ACE inhibitor therapy compared to other classes of antihypertensives. raw_response: I agree with the assessment provided by @Agent1 regarding the selection of option B - ACE inhibitors/enalapril being unsafe during pregnancy. One additional point supports this view; several large-scale epidemiological studies consistently show an increased incidence of specific malformations associated with fetal exposure to ACE inhibitor therapy compared to other classes of antihypertensives. These findings further reinforce the recommendation against their use throughout gestation unless no safer alternative exists and there's compelling need based on individual patient circumstances. Hence my agreement with Option B remaining correct here too. Answer Choice: B.
